name: egress_control_v1
description: adapterOS egress control and network isolation training data: Unix Domain Socket communication patterns, egress violation detection and blocking, legitimate vs illegitimate network activity classification, defense-in-depth enforcement layers, and migration patterns for converting networked services to UDS-only architecture.

entries.notes: 1500 examples across five categories: uds_communication_patterns (300), egress_violation_scenarios (400, label: negative), legitimate_vs_illegitimate (300), enforcement_layers (300), migration_patterns (200). All examples grounded in the actual adapterOS egress policy engine, isolation pack, PF rule validation, and UDS transport code.

provenance.review_notes: All socket paths, policy pack IDs, error types, and enforcement mechanisms sourced directly from the adapterOS codebase. Egress policy uses PolicyPackId::Egress with deny_all mode. UDS paths follow var/run/aos/<tenant>/*.sock convention. PF validation via pfctl. Network isolation uses NetworkIsolation config with allowed/blocked interfaces. Model server client supports UDS transport via tonic gRPC over UnixStream.

evaluation_gates: All UDS paths follow the canonical var/run/aos/<tenant>/*.sock pattern
Egress violations reference AosError::EgressViolation or AosError::PolicyViolation error types
Policy pack references use PolicyPackId::Egress with deny_all mode
Socket permissions are 0o660 or stricter with correct tenant UID/GID ownership
PF rule validation references pfctl -s info and pfctl -s rules commands
Compile-time enforcement correctly identifies reqwest/hyper client as prohibited dependencies
Runtime enforcement references seccomp/sandbox profile blocking connect() to non-UDS
Audit layer examples include stack trace capture for egress attempts
Negative examples (violations) always include detection mechanism and blocking response
Migration examples show complete before/after with pre-fetch and local cache patterns
All socket lifecycle examples cover creation, binding, listening, accepting, and cleanup
Telemetry export examples use var/run/metrics.sock UDS path
intent: Teach adapters the complete egress control model of adapterOS: why zero network egress is enforced, how all IPC flows over Unix Domain Sockets, how violations are detected and blocked at compile-time, runtime, and audit layers, how to distinguish legitimate local communication from prohibited network access, and how to migrate networked services to the UDS-only architecture.

subcategories.uds_communication_patterns: How all adapterOS inter-process communication works over Unix Domain Sockets. Covers socket path conventions (var/run/aos/<tenant>/*.sock), file permissions (0o660, tenant UID/GID), connection lifecycle (bind, listen, accept, read/write, close), gRPC-over-UDS for model server, metrics export via var/run/metrics.sock, and worker health probes via UnixStream.
subcategories.egress_violation_scenarios: Negative examples where components attempt prohibited network access. Adapter HTTP calls, training pipeline dataset downloads, telemetry TCP export, DNS resolution attempts, model weight remote fetches, webhook callbacks. Each violation is detected by at least one enforcement layer and results in AosError::EgressViolation, request rejection, and audit logging with stack trace.
subcategories.legitimate_vs_illegitimate: Classification of network activity as permitted or prohibited. Reverse proxy handling external TCP (legitimate), local model server gRPC over UDS (legitimate), adapter issuing HTTP request (violation), local collector receiving metrics over UDS (legitimate), training worker connecting to remote API (violation). Tests boundary reasoning.
subcategories.enforcement_layers: Defense-in-depth enforcement: compile-time (no reqwest/hyper client in Cargo.toml, feature flags gate network dependencies), runtime (seccomp/sandbox profile blocks connect() to AF_INET/AF_INET6 sockets, PF firewall deny-all egress rules validated via pfctl), audit (egress attempts logged with stack trace, threat detection engine tracks egress-spike patterns, SecurityResponseEngine quarantines violating resources).
subcategories.migration_patterns: Converting networked services to UDS-only architecture. Pre-fetching remote resources to local filesystem, replacing HTTP clients with UDS transports, configuring reverse proxies to terminate TCP and forward over UDS, local caching strategies for model weights and datasets, converting TCP-based telemetry to UDS export, and updating Cargo.toml to remove network dependencies.
